commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `errorBody` to Anthropic thinking-recovery error traversal and adds a focused regression case for a ProviderHttpError-like error body.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +12. Total +13 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `shouldRecoverAnthropicThinkingError` does not traverse `errorBody`, while `ProviderHttpError` stores bounded provider response body text there.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof and maintainer selection among overlapping candidate PRs, not a concrete repair ClawSweeper can safely make on this branch.

target.title: fix: use Object.hasOwn instead of in operator in usage bar translator
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `key in table` matches inherited `Object.prototype` properties (`toString`, `valueOf`, `constructor`) instead of only own properties.

## Why This Change Was Made

Replace `key in table` with `Object.hasOwn(table, key)` in alias lookup and case table lookup.

## User Impact

Fixes incorrect usage bar rendering when keys coincide with prototype property names.

## Evidence

```
src/auto-reply/usage-bar/translator.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  15 passed (15)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.65s
```

Includes 2 new regression tests:
- `toString` with explicit alias entry resolves correctly (not Object.prototype)
- `constructor` without alias passes through instead of hitting Object.prototype
- Map case `toString`/`constructor` fall back to `_default` correctly
